---
title: "initialise_a_ts_archive"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{initialise_a_ts_archive"}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Context:
<!-- TODO: no reference to gha, no deloRean, etc. just teach people to process data, with roxygen -->
You are a hiwi, you need to do the data processing - you know the source, etc. here's what you're used to doing ch.kof.globalbaro -> show example with kofdata, httr2, and then BFS dataset, e.g.  

## How to write your `process_data` function
- the `process_data` function does not have any params 


### Download the newest version of your data 

### Process s.t. same format as existing dataset (=tsl)

done. 




<!-- TODO: minna:

- github.com/opentsi/ch.kof.globalbaro exists:
- use github url to access this repo
- dann: hol dir die neuen daten: 
- and write process data script -> make sure the final data is in vign
- either use API (httr2), kofdata
- 
 -->



```r
# get devtools to handle dependencies 
# more serious roxygen documentation

@return tsl
@param key time series key 
@import kofdata get_timeseries
process_data <- function(key){
  tsl <- get_timeseries(key)$key
  return tsl
}

tsl <- process_data(key)

ts.plot(tsl)

```

### redo with API, instead of kofdata
